description_long: This is a long, linear fence constructed to resemble a stacked log or timber barrier, commonly seen in urban parks or as a decorative boundary in residential areas. The fence consists of a series of vertical posts evenly spaced along its length, with horizontal rails made to look like interwoven logs or branches. The top edge of the fence is relatively flat, with the tops of the posts slightly protruding above the rails. The surface texture is rough, mimicking the irregularity of natural wood or stone, and the color is a muted, earthy greenish-brown. There are no visible gates, hinges, or decorative embellishments, giving the fence a utilitarian and rustic appearance. The structure appears solid and heavy, likely intended as a fixed boundary rather than a movable barrier.
description: A long, rustic fence with a stacked log appearance, vertical posts, and a flat top edge, finished in an earthy greenish-brown color. The surface is rough and naturalistic, with no decorative elements or gates.
description_view_0: The fence is seen from the narrow end, showing only the thin profile of the posts and rails.
description_view_1: The full length of the fence is visible, displaying the stacked log pattern, evenly spaced posts, and flat top edge.
description_view_2: Another narrow end view, similar to view 0, showing the slim profile of the fence.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the fence is shown, again revealing the full length, stacked log texture, and evenly spaced posts.
